---
title: "Multi-stage recovery yield prediction"
subtitle: "Joint Monte Carlo plus time-decay weighting, deployed end-to-end on Posit Connect Cloud."
author: "Sanjin Hosic"
date: today
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, theme/sanjinhosic.scss]
    width: 1280
    height: 720
    slide-number: c/t
    progress: true
    history: true
    transition: fade
    background-transition: fade
    incremental: false
    fig-align: center
    fig-format: svg
    fig-width: 9
    fig-height: 4.4
    embed-resources: true
    code-line-numbers: false
    menu: false
    footer: "Posit Connect MLOps Demo"
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
knitr:
  opts_chunk:
    dev: svg
    dev.args:
      bg: transparent
---

```{r setup}
#| include: false
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr)
  library(tidyr)
  library(tibble)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(scales)
  library(purrr)
  library(knitr)
  library(patchwork)
})

source(here::here("R", "simulator.R"))
source(here::here("R", "recovery_model.R"))

# Palette (must match slides SCSS)
navy_bg   <- "#0a192f"
slate_dim <- "#475569"
slate_mut <- "#8892b0"
slate_bod <- "#ccd6f6"
slate_lt  <- "#a8b2d1"
accent    <- "#64ffda"
white     <- "#e6f1ff"

deck_theme <- function(base_size = 13) {
  theme_minimal(base_size = base_size) +
    theme(
      text             = element_text(colour = slate_bod, family = "Inter"),
      panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),
      plot.background  = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),
      panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      axis.title       = element_text(colour = slate_mut, size = base_size),
      axis.title.x     = element_text(margin = margin(t = 10)),
      axis.title.y     = element_text(margin = margin(r = 10)),
      axis.text        = element_text(colour = slate_lt, size = base_size - 1),
      axis.line        = element_line(colour = slate_mut, linewidth = 0.35),
      axis.ticks       = element_line(colour = slate_mut, linewidth = 0.35),
      axis.ticks.length = unit(3, "pt"),
      strip.text       = element_text(colour = slate_bod, hjust = 0, face = "bold",
                                      size = base_size, margin = margin(b = 4)),
      strip.background = element_blank(),
      legend.position  = "top",
      legend.justification = "left",
      legend.title     = element_blank(),
      legend.text      = element_text(colour = slate_bod, size = base_size - 1),
      legend.background = element_rect(fill = NA, colour = NA),
      legend.key       = element_rect(fill = NA, colour = NA),
      legend.key.width = unit(20, "pt"),
      plot.margin      = margin(8, 12, 8, 12),
      plot.title       = element_blank(),
      plot.subtitle    = element_blank(),
      plot.caption     = element_blank()
    )
}

# Generate the synthetic pool ONCE
set.seed(42)
batches <- generate_recovery_batches(n_batches = 500)
model   <- new_recovery_model(batches, reference_date = max(batches$mfg_date))

# Helpers for slide 3 (joint vs independent sampling)
chain_yield_local <- function(input_cells, r1, r2, r3, caps) {
  s1 <- pmin(pmin(input_cells, caps$input) * r1, caps$stage1)
  s2 <- pmin(s1 * r2, caps$stage2)
  s2 * r3
}
```

## Predict final yield for a new batch from a pool of historical multi-stage recoveries {#sec-setup}

[01 / Setup]{.tracker}

::: {.kicker}
Each completed batch (faint slate) passes through three sequential stages with denominator caps. The cyan line is the cohort median.
:::

```{r}
#| fig-width: 11
#| fig-height: 4.0
batch_long <- batches %>%
  select(batch_id, input_cells, stage1_output, stage2_output, stage3_output) %>%
  pivot_longer(c(input_cells, stage1_output, stage2_output, stage3_output),
               names_to = "stage", values_to = "cells") %>%
  mutate(
    stage_num   = case_when(
      stage == "input_cells"   ~ 0L,
      stage == "stage1_output" ~ 1L,
      stage == "stage2_output" ~ 2L,
      stage == "stage3_output" ~ 3L
    ),
    stage_label = factor(stage_num,
                         levels = 0:3,
                         labels = c("Input cells",
                                    "After stage 1",
                                    "After stage 2",
                                    "Final yield"))
  )

caps_df <- data.frame(
  stage_label = factor(c("Input cells", "After stage 1", "After stage 2"),
                       levels = levels(batch_long$stage_label)),
  cap = c(5e9, 3e9, 1e9)
)

ggplot(batch_long, aes(x = stage_label, y = cells)) +
  geom_line(aes(group = batch_id),
            alpha = 0.08, colour = slate_mut, linewidth = 0.35) +
  geom_hline(data = caps_df, aes(yintercept = cap),
             linetype = "dashed", colour = slate_dim, linewidth = 0.35) +
  stat_summary(aes(group = 1), fun = median, geom = "line",
               colour = accent, linewidth = 1.4) +
  stat_summary(aes(group = 1), fun = median, geom = "point",
               colour = accent, size = 3.4) +
  annotate("text",
           x = c(1, 2, 3), y = c(5e9, 3e9, 1e9),
           hjust = 1.05, vjust = -0.55,
           label = c("cap 5e9", "cap 3e9", "cap 1e9"),
           family = "JetBrains Mono", colour = slate_dim, size = 3.6) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = label_number(scale = 1e-9, suffix = "B"),
                     limits = c(0, 5.8e9), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Cells per batch") +
  deck_theme(base_size = 13)
```

[Source: R/simulator.R · seed 42 · n = 500 batches over 24 months]{.source}


## Joint resampling of whole batches preserves real covariance; independent marginal sampling falsely narrows the predictive bands {#sec-joint}

[02 / Methodology]{.tracker}

::: {.kicker}
At 2e9 input cells, sampling stage ratios independently (slate) understates predictive spread by ignoring positive within-batch correlation. Joint sampling (cyan) preserves the wider, more honest distribution.
:::

```{r}
#| fig-width: 11
#| fig-height: 4.0
set.seed(42)
input_val <- 2e9
n_draw    <- 6000

# Joint draws (matched per-batch r1, r2, r3 triples)
idx_j <- sample.int(model$n_pool, n_draw, replace = TRUE, prob = model$weights)
r1_j  <- model$r1[idx_j]
r2_j  <- model$r2[idx_j]
r3_j  <- model$r3[idx_j]
y_joint <- chain_yield_local(input_val, r1_j, r2_j, r3_j, model$caps)

# Independent draws (each stage's ratio shuffled separately)
idx1 <- sample.int(model$n_pool, n_draw, replace = TRUE, prob = model$weights)
idx2 <- sample.int(model$n_pool, n_draw, replace = TRUE, prob = model$weights)
idx3 <- sample.int(model$n_pool, n_draw, replace = TRUE, prob = model$weights)
y_ind <- chain_yield_local(input_val, model$r1[idx1], model$r2[idx2], model$r3[idx3], model$caps)

w_joint <- diff(quantile(y_joint, c(0.025, 0.975)))
w_ind   <- diff(quantile(y_ind,   c(0.025, 0.975)))
ind_narrower_pct <- round(100 * (1 - w_ind / w_joint))

bands <- bind_rows(
  tibble(method = "Independent marginals (biased)",
         q025 = quantile(y_ind, 0.025),
         q25  = quantile(y_ind, 0.25),
         q50  = median(y_ind),
         q75  = quantile(y_ind, 0.75),
         q975 = quantile(y_ind, 0.975)),
  tibble(method = "Joint sampling (correct)",
         q025 = quantile(y_joint, 0.025),
         q25  = quantile(y_joint, 0.25),
         q50  = median(y_joint),
         q75  = quantile(y_joint, 0.75),
         q975 = quantile(y_joint, 0.975))
) %>%
  mutate(method = factor(method,
                         levels = c("Independent marginals (biased)",
                                    "Joint sampling (correct)")))

ggplot(bands, aes(y = method, colour = method)) +
  geom_linerange(aes(xmin = q025, xmax = q975),
                 linewidth = 4, alpha = 0.35) +
  geom_linerange(aes(xmin = q25, xmax = q75),
                 linewidth = 11) +
  geom_point(aes(x = q50),
             size = 5.5, shape = 21, fill = navy_bg, stroke = 2) +
  geom_text(aes(x = q025,
                label = scales::label_number(scale = 1e-8, accuracy = 0.1, suffix = "e8")(q025)),
            hjust = 1.2, family = "JetBrains Mono",
            colour = slate_mut, size = 4) +
  geom_text(aes(x = q975,
                label = scales::label_number(scale = 1e-8, accuracy = 0.1, suffix = "e8")(q975)),
            hjust = -0.2, family = "JetBrains Mono",
            colour = slate_mut, size = 4) +
  annotate("text", x = max(bands$q975) * 1.18, y = 1.5,
           label = sprintf("Independent band\n≈ %d%% narrower\n(artificially)",
                           ind_narrower_pct),
           hjust = 0, vjust = 0.5,
           family = "JetBrains Mono", colour = slate_mut, size = 4,
           lineheight = 1.15) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = label_number(scale = 1e-8, suffix = "e8"),
                     expand = expansion(mult = c(0.10, 0.30))) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c(
    "Independent marginals (biased)" = slate_mut,
    "Joint sampling (correct)"       = accent
  )) +
  labs(x = "Predicted final yield at 2e9 input cells (95% and 50% intervals; dot = median)",
       y = NULL) +
  deck_theme(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.text.y = element_text(colour = slate_bod, size = 13, face = "bold"),
        axis.line.y = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.y = element_blank())
```

[Source: R/recovery_model.R `predict_yield` · n_compound = 6000 · independent variant shuffles r1, r2, r3 idx separately]{.source}


## Stage 3 recovery drifts ~6 pp/yr, so a uniform bootstrap predicts the old process, not the current one {#sec-drift}

[03 / Drift]{.tracker}

::: {.kicker}
Stage 3 recovery (cyan trend) climbs over the 24-month pool, while stages 1 and 2 stay flat. A uniformly weighted bootstrap inherits the older, lower-yield regime.
:::

```{r}
#| fig-width: 11
#| fig-height: 4.0
batches_r <- batches %>%
  mutate(
    r1 = stage1_output / pmin(input_cells,   5e9),
    r2 = stage2_output / pmin(stage1_output, 3e9),
    r3 = stage3_output / pmin(stage2_output, 1e9)
  ) %>%
  select(mfg_date, r1, r2, r3) %>%
  pivot_longer(c(r1, r2, r3), names_to = "stage", values_to = "ratio") %>%
  mutate(stage = factor(stage,
                        levels = c("r1", "r2", "r3"),
                        labels = c("Stage 1 recovery",
                                   "Stage 2 recovery",
                                   "Stage 3 recovery")))

ggplot(batches_r, aes(x = mfg_date, y = ratio)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.18, size = 0.9, colour = slate_mut) +
  geom_smooth(aes(colour = stage), method = "loess", se = FALSE,
              linewidth = 1.1, show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("Stage 1 recovery" = slate_mut,
                                 "Stage 2 recovery" = slate_mut,
                                 "Stage 3 recovery" = accent)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = label_percent(accuracy = 1)) +
  scale_x_date(date_labels = "%Y-%m", date_breaks = "6 months") +
  facet_wrap(~ stage, ncol = 3) +
  labs(x = "Manufacturing date", y = "Stage recovery ratio") +
  deck_theme(base_size = 13) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1))
```

[Source: derived from `stageN_output / pmin(stageN-1_output, cap)` per batch]{.source}


## Exponential time-decay weighting (τ = 180 days) lets recent batches dominate, tracking drift without retraining {#sec-decay}

[04 / Weighting]{.tracker}

::: {.kicker}
Left: weight curves for several τ values, with τ = 180 days highlighted. Right: under τ = 180, just the newest ~80 batches capture half the bootstrap selection mass.
:::

```{r}
#| fig-width: 11
#| fig-height: 4.0
age_grid <- seq(0, 730, by = 5)
tau_vals <- c(30, 90, 180, 360)

weight_curves <- expand.grid(age = age_grid, tau = tau_vals) %>%
  mutate(weight = exp(-age / tau),
         tau_label = factor(sprintf("τ = %d days", tau),
                            levels = c("τ = 30 days",
                                       "τ = 90 days",
                                       "τ = 180 days",
                                       "τ = 360 days")))

# Uniform baseline as a flat line
uniform_df <- data.frame(age = age_grid, weight = 1,
                         tau_label = factor("uniform (τ = ∞)",
                                            levels = c("uniform (τ = ∞)")))

p_left <- ggplot() +
  geom_line(data = uniform_df, aes(x = age, y = weight),
            colour = "#3c4d6e", linewidth = 0.7, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_line(data = weight_curves %>% filter(tau != 180),
            aes(x = age, y = weight, group = tau_label, colour = tau_label),
            linewidth = 0.85) +
  geom_line(data = weight_curves %>% filter(tau == 180),
            aes(x = age, y = weight),
            colour = accent, linewidth = 1.6) +
  annotate("text", x = 180, y = exp(-1) + 0.02,
           label = "τ = 180 days",
           hjust = 0, vjust = 0,
           family = "JetBrains Mono", colour = accent, size = 4) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c(
    "τ = 30 days"  = slate_dim,
    "τ = 90 days"  = slate_mut,
    "τ = 360 days" = slate_lt
  ), guide = "none") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(0, 180, 360, 540, 720)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1.05), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  labs(x = "Batch age (days)", y = "Weight wᵢ = exp(-age/τ)") +
  deck_theme(base_size = 13)

batches_w <- batches %>%
  mutate(age = as.numeric(max(mfg_date) - mfg_date),
         w   = exp(-age / 180),
         w   = w / sum(w)) %>%
  arrange(desc(mfg_date)) %>%
  mutate(rank     = row_number(),
         cum_mass = cumsum(w))

half_rank <- batches_w$rank[which.max(batches_w$cum_mass >= 0.5)]

p_right <- ggplot(batches_w, aes(x = rank, y = cum_mass)) +
  geom_line(colour = accent, linewidth = 1.4) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", colour = slate_dim, linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = half_rank, linetype = "dotted", colour = slate_mut, linewidth = 0.4) +
  annotate("text", x = half_rank + 8, y = 0.5,
           label = sprintf("50%% mass in newest %d batches", half_rank),
           hjust = 0, vjust = -0.8,
           family = "JetBrains Mono", colour = accent, size = 4) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 500, by = 100)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = label_percent(accuracy = 1),
                     limits = c(0, 1.02), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  labs(x = "Batches sorted newest to oldest", y = "Cumulative selection mass") +
  deck_theme(base_size = 13)

(p_left + p_right + plot_layout(ncol = 2)) &
  theme(plot.background  = element_rect(fill = NA, colour = NA),
        panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA, colour = NA))
```

[Source: weight curves analytic, cumulative mass computed on the actual 500-batch pool]{.source}


## Tighter decay shrinks effective sample size; τ = 180 days sits in the bias and variance sweet spot {#sec-ess}

[05 / Tradeoff]{.tracker}

::: {.kicker}
Effective sample size ESS = 1 / Σwᵢ² drops as decay tightens. At τ = 180 days the pool of 500 batches retains roughly 235 effective observations.
:::

```{r}
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 4.2
ages_real <- as.numeric(max(batches$mfg_date) - batches$mfg_date)

tau_grid <- c(seq(14, 90, length.out = 12),
              seq(100, 1000, length.out = 24))

ess_df <- tibble(tau = tau_grid,
                 ess = map_dbl(tau_grid, function(tau) {
                   w <- exp(-ages_real / tau)
                   w <- w / sum(w)
                   1 / sum(w^2)
                 }))

w_180 <- exp(-ages_real / 180); w_180 <- w_180 / sum(w_180)
ess_180 <- 1 / sum(w_180^2)
hl <- data.frame(tau = 180, ess = ess_180)

ggplot(ess_df, aes(x = tau, y = ess)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = nrow(batches), colour = slate_dim,
             linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.35) +
  annotate("text", x = 14, y = nrow(batches), hjust = 0, vjust = -0.7,
           label = sprintf("uniform (τ → ∞):  ESS = %d", nrow(batches)),
           family = "JetBrains Mono", colour = slate_dim, size = 3.6) +
  geom_line(colour = slate_mut, linewidth = 1.0) +
  geom_point(data = hl, aes(x = tau, y = ess),
             colour = accent, size = 5.5) +
  annotate("text", x = 180, y = ess_180,
           label = sprintf("τ = 180 days\nESS = %.0f", ess_180),
           hjust = -0.18, vjust = 0.5,
           family = "JetBrains Mono", colour = accent, size = 4.4,
           lineheight = 1.0) +
  scale_x_log10(breaks = c(14, 30, 90, 180, 360, 720),
                labels = c("14", "30", "90", "180", "360", "720")) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 500, by = 100),
                     limits = c(0, 550), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  labs(x = "τ (days, log scale)", y = "Effective sample size") +
  deck_theme(base_size = 13)
```

[Source: ESS computed on actual 500-batch pool's age distribution]{.source}


## Posit Connect Cloud + GitHub Actions + pins delivers versioned artifacts, scheduled retraining, and live serving {#sec-deploy}

[06 / Deployment]{.tracker}

::: {.kicker}
The simulator publishes a primitives pin; GitHub Actions runs three scheduled Quarto jobs (train weekly, score daily, metrics weekly) writing model, predictions, and metrics pins; two Shiny apps on Posit Connect Cloud read those pins via `pins::board_url()` from GitHub raw.
:::

![](assets/architecture.png){width="80%" fig-align="center"}

[Source: architecture as deployed; see `.github/workflows/{train,score,metrics}.yml`]{.source}


## Two live apps and the full source are available for hands-on exploration {#sec-outro}

[07 / Try it]{.tracker}

```{=html}
<div class="link-card-grid">
  <a href="https://019e1a24-eacf-d91b-b0be-ca27f3127c5e.share.connect.posit.cloud/" class="link-card" target="_blank" rel="noopener">
    <div class="link-card-label">Predictor</div>
    <div class="link-card-title">Live what-if Shiny app</div>
    <div class="link-card-cta">Open <span class="arrow">&rarr;</span></div>
  </a>
  <a href="https://019e1a33-702d-b0e0-d690-c7f82425c015.share.connect.posit.cloud/" class="link-card" target="_blank" rel="noopener">
    <div class="link-card-label">Monitoring</div>
    <div class="link-card-title">Calibration plus drift dashboard</div>
    <div class="link-card-cta">Open <span class="arrow">&rarr;</span></div>
  </a>
  <a href="https://github.com/sanjinhosic/personal-posit-mlops-demo" class="link-card" target="_blank" rel="noopener">
    <div class="link-card-label">Source</div>
    <div class="link-card-title">GitHub repository</div>
    <div class="link-card-cta">Open <span class="arrow">&rarr;</span></div>
  </a>
</div>
```

[Source: clone, install deps from .Rprofile / sessionInfo, run scripts/01_generate_primitives.R through 04_compute_metrics.R]{.source}
